t_ch: （应该会有人需要这个吧……）
t_en: (Somebody might need this...)

t_ch: 我找到了逻辑语言中枢…
t_en: I found the language module...

t_ch: 哇，这就是我一直在找的零件！只要有了这个……
t_en: Wow, this is just the part I've been looking for! As long as I have this...

t_ch: 那个，虽然这么说有点不合适…但你可以把它让给我吗？求你了，它对我来说真的很重要！
t_en: Um, it's awkward to say, but... Could you possibly let me have that? Please, it really would mean a lot to me!

t_ch: 拿去吧。
t_en: Take it.

t_ch: 这可不是白给的…
t_en: It comes with strings attached...

t_ch: …我想也是，那你出个价吧！我尽量凑钱给你……
t_en: ...That makes sense. Name your price, then! I'll do my best to put some money together for you...

t_ch: 开玩笑的，送给你了。
t_en: Just kidding. It's yours.

t_ch: 等你做好了之后再说。
t_en: We'll see, once you've finished.

t_ch: …谢谢，你真好！那我就先收下了。这元件肯定能派上用场。
t_en: ...Thank you! You're so kind! Then I'll hold onto that for you. This component will definitely come in handy.

t_ch: 不过，既然要打造能够和人进行「情感互动」的机器人，光是有这样一个模块还是不够的……
t_en: But if we want to build an emotionally interactive robot, a simple module like this won't be enough...

t_ch: ——没错，我们还得把「情感」写入其中才行！要是你们有时间了，请一定要来帮帮我呀。
t_en: ...That's right, we have to code emotion into it! If you have the time, please come and give me a hand.

t_ch: 您好，{NICKNAME}。我正在收集可用于「情感互动」的数据集。
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. I am gathering datasets that can be used for emotional interactivity.

t_ch: 我的主人菲多拉称您能为我提供合适的数据集——在学习过程中，我会为您随机预设一社交情景，您只需根据经验作答即可。
t_en: My master Fidora said that you can provide me with a suitable dataset. During the learning process, I will randomly present you with a social interaction. Please answer based on your personal experiences.

t_ch: 您输入的对话将被记录，并被逐字解析。
t_en: Your conversation will be recorded and parsed word-by-word.

t_ch: 得慎重选择了…
t_en: I'll have to choose carefully...

t_ch: 气氛好像有点紧张…
t_en: Things are starting to get a little tense...

t_ch: 即将正式进入学习环节。您准备好了吗？
t_en: Initiating learning phase: Are you ready?

t_ch: 准备好了。
t_en: Ready.

t_ch: 等、等一下…
t_en: H—Hang on...

t_ch: 您可以随时回到这里协助训练。再见！
t_en: You can come back here any time to practice. Goodbye!

t_ch: 对话情景一。
t_en: Scenario 1.

t_ch: 菲多拉最近心情很好，她邀请我去搏击俱乐部观看比赛。
t_en: Fidora has been in high spirits lately, and she invites me to watch a bout at the Fight Club.

t_ch: 在菲多拉支持的拳手挥出致胜一拳后，她看向我，并期待着我说些什么。
t_en: After the winning punch from the boxer Fidora is backing, she looks at me in anticipation of me saying something.

t_ch: 作为她的朋友，我此时的回复应该是？
t_en: As her friend, what should my reply be at this time?

t_ch: 再描述一下对话情景吧。
t_en: Describe the scenario for me once more.

t_ch: 刚才那拳真是精彩！
t_en: What an amazing punch that was!

t_ch: 他就这水平也能打拳？
t_en: Is that all you need to become a fighter?

t_ch: 你的饮料洒在我铁皮上了。
t_en: You spilled some drink on my chrome.

t_ch: 学习中……
t_en: Learning...

t_ch: 学习完成。即将进入下一个场景。
t_en: Learning complete. Initiating next scenario.

t_ch: 菲多拉最近学会了一首新歌。通过音频比对，我发现她的歌声与原曲差异很大。
t_en: Fidora has recently learned a new song. Through comparative audio analysis, I've discovered that her vocals are very different from the original.

t_ch: 即便如此，菲多拉总是不自觉地哼唱起这首歌，突然有一天，她问我自己唱得是否好听。
t_en: Even so, Fidora always hums along to this song involuntarily. One day, she suddenly asks me whether her singing is good or not.

t_ch: 作为她的朋友，请问我这个时候该说什么？
t_en: As her friend, what should I say at such a time?

t_ch: 真好听，你能教教我吗？
t_en: It's brilliant. Can you teach me?

t_ch: 你就这水平也敢唱歌？
t_en: That's the extent of your singing?

t_ch: 检测到超过正常水平的噪音。
t_en: Abnormal noise levels detected.

t_ch: 菲多拉最近发现，巴列维在卖几件从未见过的古代遗物，价格昂贵。
t_en: Fidora has recently discovered that Balaway is selling several never-before-seen ancient relics at astronomical prices.

t_ch: 菲多拉既想买下来，又想省钱。纠结之际，她希望我能出出主意。
t_en: Fidora wants to buy things, but also wants to save money. At this frustrating juncture, she hopes you can come up with an idea.

t_ch: 作为她的朋友，请问我这个时候该说什么？
t_en: As her friend, what should I say at such a time?

t_ch: 我可以为你分担一部分！
t_en: I can split it with you!

t_ch: 哈，你钱包里还剩几个子儿？
t_en: Save it. How much do you have left in your purse?

t_ch: 可用余额不足。
t_en: Insufficient funds available.

t_ch: 你按照自己的风格，帮助它完成了大量情景对话……
t_en: Using your own style, you help it get through a ton of scenarios...

t_ch: 这样就大功告成了！
t_en: You crushed it!

t_ch: 感谢你！它学到了不少临场反应，说不定真的能和人正常交流呢。
t_en: Thank you! It learned lots of on-the-spot reactions. Perhaps it really can communicate with people normally.

t_ch: 结果到底会怎么样呢…好期待啊！
t_en: How is everything going to turn out... I can't wait to see!

t_ch: 训练模式已结束，请稍后再试。
t_en: Training module finished. Please try again later.

t_ch: 外来者，终于等到你了！
t_en: Outsider, you're finally here!

t_ch: 我的机器人从你这里学会如何说话之后，确实进步飞快。
t_en: My robot improved in leaps and bounds after learning how to speak from you.

t_ch: 它的说话方式…该怎么说呢？有点热情却又不失克制，遇到问题总会第一时间为我考虑…如果要交朋友，这种性格应该就是不二之选了吧！
t_en: The way it speaks... How to put it? A little enthusiastic, but restrained. Always putting me first whenever we run into any problem... If I were making new friends, this would be the kind of personality I'd want!

t_ch: 总之，它的训练结果十分完美——谢谢你，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: All in all, its training went perfectly — thank you so much, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 不过，它的说话的方式实在是…太令人尴尬了，而且还总喜欢抬杠……
t_en: However, the way it speaks is kind of... awkward, and it loves to argue...

t_ch: 它总是自信满满地讲一些伤人的话…一两次我还能当它开玩笑，但是太过频繁了就……
t_en: It's always saying these cruel jokes at my expense... Once or twice would be okay, but it's just too much...

t_ch: 这…不是很有趣吗？
t_en: Isn't that... Fun?

t_ch: 你似乎不太满意。
t_en: You don't seem very happy about it.

t_ch: 也许是我自己的问题，但我真的不太能接受……
t_en: Perhaps it's just me, but I can't really take it...

t_ch: 哎，不管怎么样，它的训练也算上有所成果——谢谢你，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Ah, its training has paid off in any case — thank you, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 我的机器人从你这里学会如何说话之后，似乎毫无进步……
t_en: My robot didn't seem to make any improvements after learning how to speak from you...

t_ch: 它的说话方式还是那么生硬，完全不懂得变通，没有一点儿正常社交的意思。
t_en: It still speaks so bluntly, doesn't know how to adapt at all, and doesn't convey any real sense of social interaction.

t_ch: 这…不得从互相了解开始吗？
t_en: Shouldn't... things start out by getting to know each other first?

t_ch: 那也得它有这个意愿才行啊……
t_en: Then it has to have the will to do so...

t_ch: 我只是在怀疑，他是不是什么都没学到？难道是零件出故障了？又也许是学习的样本数不够……
t_en: I was just wondering, did it not learn anything? Was that a mechanical failure? Or maybe it didn't study enough samples...

t_ch: 哎，不管怎么样，它的训练也算上有所成果——谢谢你，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Ah, its training has paid off in any case — thank you, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 我的机器人从你这里学会如何说话之后，确实进步飞快……
t_en: My robot improved in leaps and bounds after learning how to speak from you...

t_ch: 它的说话方式该怎么说呢…有点让人琢磨不透。
t_en: The way it speaks is just... a little cryptic.

t_ch: 它的言谈举止非常分裂，有时候盛气凌人，有时又特别温柔，偶尔还会对我不理不睬……
t_en: It's very disjointed when it speaks, sometimes domineering but sometimes gentle — and sometimes even ignoring me...

t_ch: 这…不挺丰富的吗？
t_en: Haven't those been an abundant variety of experiences?

t_ch: 我觉得可能是底层系统出现了问题，导致它有了不稳定的倾向。
t_en: I think there may be a problem with the core systems causing it to lose stability.

t_ch: 不过作为朋友，能保持一定的新鲜感倒也不是什么坏事……
t_en: But as a friend, it's not always a bad thing to have such fresh opinions...

t_ch: 现在比较流行这种随机感。
t_en: Being spontaneous is totally in right now.

t_ch: …人类反而做不到这样。
t_en: ...Humans can't do that.

t_ch: 哎，或许你说得对。总之——谢谢你，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Hey, maybe you're right. Anyway, thank you, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 你好，我是菲多拉的「情感互动」机器人，请多关照！
t_en: Hello, I'm Fidora's emotional interactive robot. Nice to meet you!

t_ch: 看什么看，没见过机器人啊？
t_en: What are you looking at? Have you never seen a robot before?

t_ch: 系统提示：你正处在本机预设的行进路线上，请及时避让，以免发生危险。
t_en: System prompt: You are standing on an intended travel pathway. Please step aside to avoid any harm.

t_ch: 看什么看？█ █ ██错误，错误█ ██ █你好，请多关照！
t_en: What are you looking at? █ █ ██ Error, error █ ██ █ Hello, nice to meet you!

t_ch: 我来取星轨之兔的周边…
t_en: I'm here to pick up the Star Rabbit merch...

t_ch: 哈哈，我就知道你感兴趣。认真参与「星轨之兔」的银河玩家，请看，这就是客人为您的游戏热情献上的特别纪念——
t_en: Haha, I knew you'd be interested. Attention, dedicated cosmic players of "Star Rabbit!" Behold, a special commemorative gift from our guest to celebrate your gaming passion!

t_ch: 显而易见，这上面画了一只毛茸茸的右手。很可能来自一只布偶，或者……
t_en: It's obviously a drawing of a furry right hand. It probably belongs to a stuffed toy, or...

t_ch: 这不是列车长吗？
t_en: Isn't that the conductor?

t_ch: 「星轨之兔」，莫非…
t_en: "Star Rabbit"... Could it be...

t_ch: 这我就不知道啦。那位神秘的客人只是说，「星轨之兔」是一款纪念烬土纪元无名客们与这颗星球并肩作战的游戏。
t_en: Beats me. That mysterious guest just said that "Star Rabbit" is a game commemorating the Nameless who fought alongside this planet during the Cindearth Age.

t_ch: 设计方根据当年那些无名客留下的只言片语，在现实中投放了可以帮助大家通关游戏的尘灵助手，也用这种方式向他们表达敬意。
t_en: Based on the fragmented accounts left behind by those Nameless long ago, the developers released wispae helpers in the real world to assist everyone in beating the game. A tribute to those trailblazers, in their own way.

t_ch: 客人说，一位现代的无名客也在游玩「星轨之兔」，这让他们又惊又喜。他们决定为您准备一套特殊的礼品。
t_en: The guest says that a modern-day Nameless is also enjoying "Star Rabbit," which delights and surprises them. They've decided to prepare a special gift set for you.

t_ch: 现在您收到的只是这套礼品的第一份——随着体验「星轨之兔」的关卡越来越多，您还会收到更多类似的礼品；如果在未来能够全部收齐，或许还会有意想不到的好事发生。
t_en: What you've received is just the first piece of this gift set. As you complete more Star Rabbit stages, you'll receive additional pieces. If you manage to collect them all, something unexpectedly wonderful might just happen.

t_ch: 看来又是一场收集之旅！
t_en: Looks like another collection quest!

t_ch: 哈哈。看到您这么有兴致，那位客人应该也很开心吧。
t_en: Haha. With you showing this much enthusiasm, I bet that guest is over the moon right now.

t_ch: 对了对了，这里还有客人留下的一封信，您可以慢慢过目。我就继续忙自己的工作啦。
t_en: Oh, oh! By the way, a guest left you a letter. Feel free to take a look when you get a chance. I'll go ahead and get back to what I was doing!

t_ch: …藏着掖着，真不痛快。
t_en: ...all this sneaking around and hiding stuff, it's really killing the vibe.

t_ch: 我们的这位客人比较害羞啦。如果时机成熟，TA肯定还是想和您见一面的。
t_en: Our guest here is a bit on the shy side. But when the time's right, they'll definitely want to meet you in person.

t_ch: 对了对了，这里还有客人留下的一封信，您可以慢慢过目。我就继续忙自己的工作啦。
t_en: Oh, oh! By the way, a guest left you a letter. Feel free to take a look when you get a chance. I'll go ahead and get back to what I was doing!

t_ch: 这套纪念卡，它值钱吗？
t_en: Is this commemorative card set worth anything of value?

t_ch: 不清楚呢。不过，在轮值酒保这里寄存私人物品可不是免费的…只要您还没有收到，那位客人每天都要向世界尽头酒馆支付<unbreak>3000</unbreak>信用点和一个原创笑话：代价还是蛮高昂的。
t_en: I'm not sure. But storing personal items with the drinksmith isn't free... Until they pick up their belongings, a customer has to pay the World's End Tavern <unbreak>3,000</unbreak> credits and one original joke each day. That's quite a steep price.

t_ch: 看在TA这么执着的份上，就算「礼轻情意重」，您想必也可以通过讲个好故事，将这套纪念卡卖出高价吧。
t_en: Given their dedication, even if "it's the thought that counts," you can surely spin a good story and sell this commemorative card set for a hefty price.

t_ch: 不管怎么说，这里还有客人留下的一封信，您可以慢慢过目。我就继续忙自己的工作啦。
t_en: Anyway, there's a letter here left by a guest. You can take your time reading it. I'll get back to my own work now.

t_ch: 不是说这人还留了一封信吗？
t_en: Didn't they say this person left a letter or something?

t_ch: 是啊！就在这里，您可以慢慢过目。我就继续忙自己的工作啦。
t_en: Yep! Right here. Take your time reading it. I'll get back to my own work now.

t_ch: 欢迎游玩「星轨之兔」！「星轨之兔」是由月眠一族赞助的超高难度闯关游戏。您可以通过在附近集齐我同类的方式来降低游戏难度，从而轻松通关~
t_en: Welcome to Star Rabbit! Star Rabbit is an ultra-challenging obstacle course sponsored by the moonlulls. You can lower the difficulty by finding other rabbits nearby, making it easier to breeze through the levels~

t_ch: 打开游戏机。
t_en: Turn on the game console.

t_ch: 加油！
t_en: You can do it!

t_ch: 关于「星轨之兔」…
t_en: About Star Rabbit...

t_ch: 「星轨之兔」的主题是「现实、努力、胜利」。您会发现我同类的言行举止，或许比普通的尘灵更加忧郁…但这正是烬土纪元古弁才天国的精神气质。
t_en: The theme of Star Rabbit is "Reality, Effort, Victory." You may find that those of my kind speak and behave in a manner that is perhaps more melancholic than ordinary wispae... but that is just the spiritual character of ancient Benzaitengoku from the Cindearth Age.

t_ch: 那时的英雄喜欢说，「适度的悲观是一种远见」。制作团队认为，基于悲观的英雄主义，这正是今天的哈托彼亚，或者说二相乐园——最稀缺的一种欢愉。
t_en: The heroes of that time liked to say, "A moderate dose of pessimism is foresight." The production team believes that this kind of heroism born from pessimism is precisely the rarest form of Elation in today's Ahatopia — or rather, Planarcadia.

t_ch: 如您所知，您可以通过在附近集齐我同类的方式来降低「星轨之兔」的游戏难度。为了给这个过程增添烬土纪元的风味，制作者对尘灵们的台词做了格言化处理。
t_en: As you know, you can reduce the difficulty of the Star Rabbit minigame by gathering my fellow wispae nearby. To add a bit of Cindearth Age flavor to the process, the creator has stylized the wispae's dialogue into aphorisms.

t_ch: 这并不代表在烬土纪元，英雄们出口就是格言…反而只有当他们慨然赴死的时候，才会如此这般去思考吧。
t_en: That's not to say heroes of the Cindearth Age spouted profound quotes left and right... Rather, they only thought this way when facing their final moments with resolve.

t_ch: 由于赞助商的存在，「星轨之兔」不必刻意迎合欢愉信徒们过于多变的时尚潮流。
t_en: Thanks to its sponsors, Star Rabbit doesn't have to chase after the ever-changing fashion trends of the Elation's followers.

t_ch: 或许尘灵们的言行与如今的二相乐园显得格格不入…但赞助商希望您能够相信，在古老的时代，沉思并不是一件会令人蒙羞的行为。
t_en: The wispae's behavior may seem out of place in modern-day Planarcadia, but the sponsors would like you to know that in ancient times, contemplation wasn't something to be ashamed of.

t_ch: 「星轨之兔」的背景设定在烬土纪元——这是在今天的二相乐园是一个经典的创作题材，但赞助商月眠一族希望制作组能为它赋予一些新意。
t_en: Star Rabbit is set against the backdrop of the Cindearth Age: a classic creative theme in today's Planarcadia. But the moonlull sponsors hope the production team can bring in some fresh ideas.

t_ch: 如今的时代剧或类似作品中，人们常常强调入画前人们的古典气息…但换个角度观察，那个时代也正是幻造种得以确立社会地位、哈托彼亚开始向现代发展的起点。
t_en: In period dramas and similar works, people often emphasize the classical atmosphere of the era before teleportation... but from another angle, that was also the very era when the imagenae established their social status and Ahatopia began its journey toward modernization.

t_ch: 基于这个视角，我们提供给您用来收集的尘灵，会有意强调它们作为现代人而非古人的侧面~
t_en: From this point of view, the wispae we're giving you to collect will deliberately highlight their modernity rather than their antiquity~

t_ch: 我要降低难度！
t_en: I'm lowering the difficulty!

t_ch: 真没办法，就向您指出我某位同类的所在之处吧。下不为例哦？
t_en: Fine, fine, I'll point you toward one of my kin. But this is a one-time deal, got it?

t_ch: 您已经在附近找齐了我的同类…请在努力在游戏中活用我们的能力哦~
t_en: You've already collected all of my kind in this area... Now put our abilities to good use, okay~?

t_ch: 打开游戏机。
t_en: Turn on the game console.

t_ch: 退出。
t_en: Exit.

t_ch: 唔…降低难度需要支付代价。你做好准备了吗？<unbreak>20</unbreak>个像素金币，换一个降低难度的线索。
t_en: Hmm... lowering the difficulty comes at a cost. Are you ready? <unbreak>20</unbreak> Pixcoins for a hint to make things easier.

t_ch: 金币给你。
t_en: Here's your Pixcoins.

t_ch: 我的像素金币不够。
t_en: I don't have enough Pixcoins.

t_ch: 哎呀。平常请留意收集吧。
t_en: Oops. Better keep an eye out for them then.

t_ch: 尘灵用一点点愿力向你的手机中注入了一条导航信息。
t_en: The wispae used a bit of Wishpower to inject navigation info into your phone.

t_ch: 这需要一步一步来。先找到我上次为您指出的某位同类吧？
t_en: Let's take this one step at a time. First, how about finding that unit I pointed out to you last time?

t_ch: 找到<unbreak>10</unbreak>个尘灵有奖励吗？
t_en: Is there a reward for finding ten wispae?

t_ch: …哦？您已经遇见了它们其中的一半？可喜可贺，请收下这份纪念奖励。
t_en: ...Oh? You've already encountered half of them? Well, well, congratulations are in order! Please accept this commemorative reward.

t_ch: 找到<unbreak>20</unbreak>个尘灵有奖励吗？
t_en: Is there a reward for finding twenty wispae?

t_ch: …哦？您已经全部遇见了它们？太厉害了，现在您游玩「星轨之兔」应该已经易如反掌~
t_en: ...Oh? You've already encountered all of them? Impressive! Playing Star Rabbit should be a piece of cake for you now~

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 慢走不送~
t_en: See ya~

t_ch: 哟，别来无恙，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Oh, hey there! Long time no see, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 事不宜迟，先把东西交给你——
t_en: Let's get down to business. Here!

t_ch: 这次是一张关于「左脚」的卡牌。
t_en: This time, it's a card featuring a "left foot."

t_ch: 之前在短信里也说了，这次跑来寄存周边的人急匆匆就走了，什么多余的话都没有说，显得有些心不在焉…也不知道他们「星轨之兔」是不是出了什么变故。唔，也可能只是人家太忙了？
t_en: Like I said in my message, the person who dropped off the merch left in a huge hurry. They didn't say a single word, almost like their mind was elsewhere... I hope everything's okay with the Star Rabbit crew. Or maybe they're just super busy?

t_ch: 忙点好啊，不用社交。
t_en: Being busy is great! No need to socialize!

t_ch: 哈哈。原来如此，原来如此。无名客做好事不留名的真正原因终于揭晓——因为不擅长社交！
t_en: Hah! Mystery solved! So that's why the Nameless stay anonymous. It's not about being humble, you're all just socially anxious!

t_ch: 咳咳，开个玩笑，别介意。我不知道他们的上封信里给你写了些什么…如果给你造成了社交疲劳，那么我觉得你还是要稍微原谅一下他们。毕竟是那是月眠一族嘛。
t_en: Ahem, just kidding! I don't know what they wrote in their last letter, but if it was a bit much for you, try to cut them some slack. They are moonlulls, after all.

t_ch: 他们里面有人仰慕无名客，那太正常不过了。
t_en: It's totally normal for them to admire the Nameless.

t_ch: 听上去是有点不对劲。
t_en: Something sounds a little fishy.

t_ch: 对吧对吧，你也有不好的预感？
t_en: Right? You've got a bad feeling, too?

t_ch: 你多心了吧？
t_en: You're probably just overthinking it.

t_ch: 真的吗…我直觉一向很准的哦。
t_en: Really? My intuition is usually spot on, you know.

t_ch: 不过话说回来，我也没有人家的联系方式，不太可能搞清楚到底发生了什么。
t_en: But I don't have any way to contact them, so I guess we'll never know what actually happened.

t_ch: 从你也一头雾水的角度来看…要想知道个中秘辛，或许要再等等吧。不管怎么说，我要继续忙自己的工作啦——你要能点杯饮料，那我就更开心啦。
t_en: Since you're in the dark too, I guess the truth will just have to wait. Anyway, I've got to get back to work. I'd be a lot happier if you could order a drink, though.

t_ch: 本作是基于赞助商搜集的民间传说定制而成。也就是说，这不是一份关于二相乐园历史事实的记录，而是一份关于二相乐园历史情绪的记录。
t_en: This work is a commissioned adaptation based on folklore assembled by our sponsors. It is, therefore, not a document of Planarcadia's historical facts, but of its historical emotional record.

t_ch: …请不要小看历史情绪。在时间的长河中，比起历史事实，历史情绪才是历史作用于现实的直接力量。也因此才有「一切历史都是当代史」的提法。
t_en: ...Please do not underestimate historical emotion. In time's long flow, it is not the cold facts but the living emotions of the past that shape our present. This is the very reason why we have the saying, "All history is contemporary history."

t_ch: 呃，我们一定要聊这个吗？好吧，这的确是我的责任之一…之前应该已经有很多尘灵给你讲过各种各样的八卦了……
t_en: Ugh, do we have to talk about this? Fine, I guess it is one of my responsibilities... You've probably already heard all kinds of gossip from the other wispae...

t_ch: 啊，有了。月眠一族一直声称自己是星空旅行家的后代——但他们从来都拒绝幻造种溯源检测，也就是说拒绝用科学方法验证此事的真假。嗯，你细品。
t_en: Ah, here it is. The moonlulls have always claimed to be descendants of starry voyagers, but they always refuse the Imagenae Retracing Test. In other words, they refuse to scientifically verify whether this claim is true or false. Hmm... just think about the implications.

t_ch: 我又找到了两页《浮脂记事》。
t_en: I found two more pages of the Floating Grease Chronicles.

t_ch: 真的？那我要趁自己还活着，赶紧一睹为快……
t_en: Really? Then I better check it out while I'm still kicking...

t_ch: 查令在手机上读到了「兔之章 其五」和「猴之章 其十一」。
t_en: Charing read "Chapter of the Rabbit (V)" and "Chapter of the Monkey (XI)" on the phone.

t_ch: …没想到我们虽然收齐了四大篇章的「其一」，后面的内容却如此残缺不全。
t_en: ...I can't believe it. Even though we managed to collect Part I for all four chapters, the rest of the content is so fragmented and incomplete.

t_ch: 本来我还希望在「其二」或者「其三」中能找到一些关于幻月诞生的蛛丝马迹…但现在看来，这套诗集的保存状况比我之前的设想要糟糕很多。
t_en: I was hoping to find some clues about the birth of the Phantasmoon in Part II or Part III... but the collection's in way worse shape than I thought.

t_ch: 至少单篇诗作还是完整的。
t_en: At least the individual poems are still intact.

t_ch: 嗯，这一点确实万幸。不过……
t_en: Yeah, that's a relief, at least. But...

t_ch: 说不定只是书页被打乱了顺序？
t_en: Maybe the pages just got shuffled around?

t_ch: 按照我之前的推理，页码越大的部分越容易从幻造书上脱落——这也是为什么我们能在离它最近的地方发现「序章」和四大篇章的「其一」——因为它们是最后脱落的。
t_en: Following my earlier deduction, the higher the page number, the more likely that section is to fall off the imagenesis book. That would explain why we found the "Prologue" and the "Part I" of the four major chapters closest to the book itself. They were the last to fall off.

t_ch: 但之后，我们找到的却已经是「兔之章 其五」和「猴之章 其十一」，这就意味着它们之间的内容，很可能在许多许多年前就已经散佚了。
t_en: But after that, what we found were the "Chapter of the Rabbit (V)" and "Chapter of the Monkey (XI)." That means the content in between had likely been lost many, many years ago.

t_ch: …不过，这或许也说明，已经很久、很久没有人试图整理、抢救《浮脂记事》了——只要我们能搜集到足够重新出版的数量，那一定会有人高价收购！
t_en: ...But hey, maybe that just means it's been ages, like eons, since anyone tried to catalog or salvage the Floating Grease Chronicles. If we can collect enough copies to reprint it, someone's bound to pay big for them!

t_ch: 呃…大概是这样吧。其实我也不确定二相乐园的人们对于古籍还有多大的热情。我的旧书店里面，销量好的还都是漫画书呢。
t_en: Uh... I guess so. Honestly, I'm not even sure how much the folks in Planarcadia still care about ancient texts. At my used bookstore, it's always comic books that sell.

t_ch: 努力总会有回报。
t_en: Hard work always pays off.

t_ch: 哈哈，借你吉言。要是机缘巧合，旧书店说不定也能用自己的方式拯救世界呢！
t_en: Haha, I'll hope that's true! Who knows? Maybe with a bit of luck, this little bookstore of mine can save the world in its own way!

t_ch: 单纯当作探险也挺好玩。
t_en: Just thinking of it as an adventure is fun too.

t_ch: 确实！对你来说，寻找书页本身就是一种探险游戏了。
t_en: Exactly! For you, finding those pages is an adventure in itself.

t_ch: 这就是所谓「时代变了」。
t_en: This is what they call "times have changed."

t_ch: 是啊。想当初，像这样的诗歌，又何尝不是那个年代的流行文化呢。
t_en: Yeah. Back in the day, poetry like this was exactly what pop culture is today.

t_ch: 这样想想，就算我们最后收集到的《浮脂记事》残破不堪，根本无法体现原作的完整面貌…它也一定会有自己的独特意义。
t_en: When you put it that way, even if what we end up with is an incomplete collection of the Floating Grease Chronicles, a far cry from the original work in its full glory... it'll still carry its own unique weight.

t_ch: 谢谢你！只要我还没死掉，那下次我们就还要继续合作哦？
t_en: Thanks! As long as I'm still alive, we'll be working together again next time, yeah?

t_ch: 裂界中一片死寂。粘滞的空气几乎在这座废弃电话亭的周围结成了块状，你感觉自己像被淹没在融化过程中的棉花糖里，喘不上气来。
t_en: All's quiet in the Fragmentum. You feel like you're drowning in gooey cotton candy in the stagnant air that seems to congeal around the phone booth.

t_ch: ——棉花糖？嗯…借着这一闪而过的比喻，你开始想象棉花糖的味道了——虽然你不确定自己是否真的尝试过这种食物。或许你该去问问那位列车长……
t_en: Cotton Candy, eh...? You begin imagining how it would taste based on the brief description, even though you're not sure if you've ever had it. Perhaps you should ask the conductor...

t_ch: 等等，那是什么声音？一阵诡异的电话铃声撕破了凝滞的空气。你脑海里的抽象棉花糖被一口吞掉，甚至没给你留下一丝回甘。
t_en: Wait, what's that sound? The phone rings, shattering the silence and ripping the cotton candy from your mind, not even leaving an aftertaste.

t_ch: 摆在你面前的只剩一个问题：接还是不接？
t_en: Only one question remains... Are you picking up the phone or not?

t_ch: 我别无选择。接它。
t_en: I got no choice but to answer.

t_ch: 不错！身为开拓者，你怎么能连这点冒险精神都没有？
t_en: Yes! No true Trailblazer should ignore such an adventure!

t_ch: 去吧，拿起那个…肮脏的听筒！听听这裂界到底想用什么故事刺激你的神经。
t_en: Now, pick up that dirty phone and hear what stimulating story the Fragmentum has in store for you!

t_ch: 不…这完全不明智。别碰它。
t_en: No... this is a bad idea. Not going to touch it.

t_ch: 感性带来的冲动悄然退场，理智宣布了它的胜利。只是又一个裂界描摹现实产生的幻象罢了，它不值得你浪费宝贵的时间。
t_en: The impulse fades and sensibility takes over. It's just another Fragmentum illusion not worth your time.

t_ch: ——或许更主要的原因还是那个肮脏的听筒。那玩意对你的听觉肯定没益处……
t_en: Or maybe it's the filthy phone. Nothing good can come out of touching it...

t_ch: 你进来了！后悔已经晚了。假如你现在逃跑，所有人都会嘲笑你的。
t_en: You're already inside and it's too late for regrets! You'll be mocked if you run away now.

t_ch: 你拿起了听筒，小心翼翼地把它贴到耳边……
t_en: You pick up the phone and carefully put it next to your ear...

t_ch: …你在哪？
t_en: ...Where are you?

t_ch: 听筒中传来一个陌生女人的声音。出乎你的意料，她的声音出奇地正常。她听上去只是有些衰弱。
t_en: You're not familiar with the female voice. Surprisingly, the voice sounds calm, just a bit weak.

t_ch: …你在哪？
t_en: ...Where are you?

t_ch: 她又重复了一遍。她在渴求回答。
t_en: She repeats, craving your answer.

t_ch: 我在…贝洛伯格的商业街上。
t_en: I'm... in the business district of Belobog.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 别回来了…我不想见到你。
t_en: Don't come back. I don't want to see you.

t_ch: 碧翠丝哭了一整晚。但我知道你根本不在乎。
t_en: Beatrice cried the whole night, but I know you don't care.

t_ch: 我累了。我要去睡一会儿…我不想再争吵了。我没有力气……
t_en: I'm tired and want to go to bed. I don't want to fight anymore... I don't have the energy...

t_ch: 她很生气…你不知道为什么。你什么都没做，但罪恶感还是像苏打水翻腾的气泡一样，在你胸腔里滋滋作响。
t_en: She's livid but you don't even know why. You didn't do anything, but guilt still filled your chest like bubbling soda water.

t_ch: …我要挂了。
t_en: ...I'm hanging up.

t_ch: 别挂！
t_en: No, don't!

t_ch: 对…对不起。
t_en: I—I'm sorry.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 你不明白。这么多年了，你从来就没真正反省过……
t_en: All these years, you never once blamed yourself...

t_ch: 你眼里就只有生意…为了那一摊子生意，你什么都能放弃……
t_en: All you do is sell... You'd give up anything for the business...

t_ch: 如果…如果你觉得我们之间还有重归于好的余地…那你真是被冲昏了头。
t_en: If... If you think we can still reconcile, you're out of your mind.

t_ch: 你终于意识到了…那个女人并不是在与你对话。她的语句间之所以留有间隙，纯粹是因为一阵又一阵的情感溃堤。
t_en: You finally realize the woman is not interested in a conversation so much as venting her frustrations.

t_ch: 那就保持沉默吧。听听她——也许是个幽灵——还有什么话想说。
t_en: Just be quiet and listen. Maybe she's a spirit with something to say.

t_ch: 我要离开了。带着碧翠丝一起…去哪儿？我不知道，也不会告诉你。但总之不是这里……
t_en: I'm leaving and taking Beatrice with me. Where to? I don't know and won't tell you, but I won't stay here anymore...

t_ch: 我真希望贝洛伯格能再大一些…那样我就不用担心会再碰见你了。
t_en: I wish Belobog could be larger, so I don't chance running into you.

t_ch: 沉默。沉默。一阵漫长到令你难以忍受的沉默。
t_en: Stay quiet, though it's becoming unbearable.

t_ch: 顺带一提，昨天是碧翠丝的五岁生日。二月十三日。
t_en: By the way, Beatrice turned five yesterday, February <unbreak>13</unbreak>.

t_ch: 记不住也没关系…反正以后也和你无关了。再见，史考——
t_en: It's okay if you don't remember, not that you need to, anymore. So long, Sco—

t_ch: 电话断线了。聒噪的「嘟嘟」声不断敲击着你空白的心灵。
t_en: The call is disconnected. The annoying beeps gnaw at your soul.

t_ch: （那个女人最后提到了一个名字…「史考」？听着不像是个完整的名字。）
t_en: (She mentioned the name "Sco—" at the end, but it didn't sound complete...)

t_ch: （还有其它值得注意的信息吗？对了，她还提到了他们的女儿…碧翠丝？而且她的生日是二月十三日…）
t_en: (Anything else worth noting... Oh, she mentioned their daughter... Beatrice? Her birthday was February <unbreak>13</unbreak>.)

t_ch: （不知道这些信息有什么意义…总之先记下来吧。）
t_en: (Don't know what that's good for, but I'll write it down, anyway.)

t_ch: 桌子上放着一只老旧的箱子，它的开口被比箱子本身看上去更为老旧的机械锁锁住。
t_en: There's an old box on the table with a combination lock that appears even older than the box.

t_ch: 桌子旁放着一张纸条，上面用潦草的字体写着：「史考特先生的箱子。没有值钱的东西。勿动」——哈！真是此地无银三百两。
t_en: Next to it is a memo with sloppy handwriting that reads, "Mr. Scott's box, containing nothing valuable inside. Do not open." Aha! He couldn't have advertised its value more clearly.

t_ch: 该不该试着打开它呢？老实说，那个史考特的态度奇差…你早就看他不爽了。
t_en: Should you open it? Frankly, you don't like Scott as it is...

t_ch: 尝试打开密码锁。
t_en: Try to figure out the combination.

t_ch: 没错，动手吧！就算发生了什么，也都是那个惹人厌的拳馆老板自找的——至少你能这么安慰自己。
t_en: Sure, do it! It's all that gym owner's fault, anyway... At least that's your excuse.

t_ch: 还是算了吧。
t_en: Never mind.

t_ch: 你强行压抑住了内心的冲动。没错，人之所以为人，就是因为我们心中有值得奉行的道德律——
t_en: You suppress the urge. That's right, we are humans because we know right from wrong...

t_ch: ——算了，别把话说的太死。也许下一秒你就改主意了呢？
t_en: Come on, don't be too sure of it. You could be having a change of heart the next second.

t_ch: 你把手放到了箱子上。这个老旧的机械锁一共有四个凹槽，每个凹槽对应一个数字。现在轮到你行动了——第一个数字是什么？
t_en: You put your hands on the box. The ancient combo lock has four slots, which corresponds to four numbers. Now it's your turn. So, what's the first number?
Zero.

t_ch: 咔哒！清脆的声音从机械锁中响起。看来你猜对了第一个数字。
t_en: *click* The lock clanged loudly and crisply. You've guessed the first number.
One.

t_ch: 错误。你听到一阵沉闷的滋滋声，四个凹槽内的数字恢复到了初始的、无序的状态。糟糕——看来你得从头再试一次了。
t_en: Wrong. You hear a low buzzing sound as the numbers in all four slots return to their original random state. Shoot, looks like you gotta start all over again.
Two.

t_ch: 错误。你听到一阵沉闷的滋滋声，四个凹槽内的数字恢复到了初始的、无序的状态。糟糕——看来你得从头再试一次了。
t_en: Wrong. You hear a low buzzing sound as the numbers in all four slots return to their original random state. Shoot, looks like you gotta start all over again.
Three.

t_ch: 错误。你听到一阵沉闷的滋滋声，四个凹槽内的数字恢复到了初始的、无序的状态。糟糕——看来你得从头再试一次了。
t_en: Wrong. You hear a low buzzing sound as the numbers in all four slots return to their original random state. Shoot, looks like you gotta start all over again.
Four.

t_ch: 错误。你听到一阵沉闷的滋滋声，四个凹槽内的数字恢复到了初始的、无序的状态。糟糕——看来你得从头再试一次了。
t_en: Wrong. You hear a low buzzing sound as the numbers in all four slots return to their original random state. Shoot, looks like you gotta start all over again.

t_ch: 干得不错——假如你刚才不是纯凭运气的话。现在，继续！该输入第二个数字了。
t_en: Good job... Assuming it wasn't sheer luck. Now, on to the second number.
Zero.

t_ch: 错误。你听到一阵沉闷的滋滋声，四个凹槽内的数字恢复到了初始的、无序的状态。糟糕——看来你得从头再试一次了。
t_en: Wrong. You hear a low buzzing sound as the numbers in all four slots return to their original random state. Shoot, looks like you gotta start all over again.
One.

t_ch: 错误。你听到一阵沉闷的滋滋声，四个凹槽内的数字恢复到了初始的、无序的状态。糟糕——看来你得从头再试一次了。
t_en: Wrong. You hear a low buzzing sound as the numbers in all four slots return to their original random state. Shoot, looks like you gotta start all over again.
Two.

t_ch: 咔哒！清脆的声音从机械锁中响起。第二道机关也被你解开了，真棒！
t_en: *click* The lock clanged loudly and crisply. You've solved the second number too. Awesome!
Three.

t_ch: 错误。你听到一阵沉闷的滋滋声，四个凹槽内的数字恢复到了初始的、无序的状态。糟糕——看来你得从头再试一次了。
t_en: Wrong. You hear a low buzzing sound as the numbers in all four slots return to their original random state. Shoot, looks like you gotta start all over again.
Four.

t_ch: 错误。你听到一阵沉闷的滋滋声，四个凹槽内的数字恢复到了初始的、无序的状态。糟糕——看来你得从头再试一次了。
t_en: Wrong. You hear a low buzzing sound as the numbers in all four slots return to their original random state. Shoot, looks like you gotta start all over again.

t_ch: 很好，工作已经完成一半了。别骄傲——冷静下来，想想第三个数字会是什么……
t_en: You're halfway there, but don't get cocky and try to figure out the third number...
Zero.

t_ch: 错误。你听到一阵沉闷的滋滋声，四个凹槽内的数字恢复到了初始的、无序的状态。糟糕——看来你得从头再试一次了。

t_en: Take your spoils. You deserve everything inside.

t_en: (Is it... Scott's handwriting?)

t_ch: 愧疚感填满了你。
t_en: Guilt overcomes you.

t_ch: 你把搏击俱乐部老板的笔记放回了箱子，然后轻轻地将它合上，将机械密码锁归位。
t_en: You put the gym owner's notes back into the box, gently close it, and lock the combination lock.

t_ch: 咔哒。就当一切都没发生过。也许这是最好的结果。
t_en: *click* Just pretending it never happened is perhaps the best conclusion.

t_ch: 这块表是你的吗？
t_en: Is this watch yours?

t_ch: 哟，这不是…西蒙勋爵给我的那块表嘛！我正纳闷这玩意跑哪去了呢，谢谢你啊。
t_en: That's the watch Lord Simon gave me as a gift! I was just wondering where it had gone. Thank you so much.

t_ch: 不给我点报酬吗？
t_en: So no recompense for returning the watch to you?

t_ch: 当然了，我华劳斯怎么会亏待朋友呢！
t_en: Why, yes, of course. I, Wallace, will never mistreat a friend!

t_ch: 你就不好奇它是怎么丢的吗？
t_en: So, you aren't even slightly curious about how it got lost?

t_ch: 不好奇啊。就是个第三任大守护者的遗物而已嘛，丢了也不是很可惜。
t_en: Not really. It's just an artifact left behind by the third Supreme Guardian. Won't be that big of a loss anyway.

t_ch: 不过，既然你帮我找到了，那我肯定要如约重谢——我华劳斯绝不会亏待朋友！
t_en: Nevertheless, since you are the one who returned it to me, I will reward you handsomely as promised. I, Wallace, will never mistreat a friend!

t_ch: 这是我一点心意，还请笑纳。
t_en: This is the reward I prepared for you. Please enjoy.

t_ch: 老板，你什么都没看见！
t_en: Boss, you didn't see anything!

t_ch: 啊，失败了吗…人们都说「一叶障目，不见幻月」，原来用两片叶子是不行的啊。我真傻，老板。
t_en: Ah, did it fail again...? They say "One leaf blinds the eyes, and you don't know where the Phantasmoon hides." Turns out two leaves don't work either. I'm such a fool, boss.

t_ch: 老板？谁是你的老板？
t_en: Boss? Who is your boss?

t_ch: 当然您是我的老板呀！我在《狸狸周刊》勤勤恳恳工作了十三年，只是进酒馆的机会太难得，这才一时鬼迷心窍……
t_en: You are, of course! I've worked hard at Furbobo Weekly for thirteen years. It's just... opportunities to sneak into the tavern are rare, and I let temptation get the better of me...

t_ch: 哦，看来你也是周刊的…
t_en: Oh, so you work for the magazine too...

t_ch: 是的老板，没错老板！我在《狸狸周刊》勤勤恳恳工作了十三年，只是进酒馆的机会太难得，这才一时鬼迷心窍……
t_en: Yes, boss! That's right, boss! I've worked hard at Furbobo Weekly for thirteen years. It's just... opportunities to sneak into the tavern are rare, and I let temptation get the better of me...

t_ch: 少废话，快回去上班。
t_en: Enough excuses. Back to work.

t_ch: 是是是，老板您最好了！我这就回去上班！
t_en: Yes, yes, you're the best, boss! I'll head back right now!

t_ch: 没事，我理解。下不为例。
t_en: Okay, I get it. Just this once.

t_ch: 啊啊啊，感谢老板慈悲为怀！下次我一定只用一片叶子——啊不是，下次我一定不敢了！我这就回去上班！
t_en: Ah, thank you for your mercy, boss! Next time I'll only use one leaf... No, wait, I mean I won't do it again! I'll go right now!

t_ch: 你被开除了！
t_en: You're fired!

t_ch: 啊啊啊，老板饶命啊！我全家老小都指着这一口饭，下次一定不敢了啊！
t_en: Ahhh, please forgive me, boss! My whole family depends on this job! I won't do it again, I swear!

t_ch: 慈悲为怀，拿捏狸心。
t_en: I'll be merciful, if only to gain furbo allegiance.

t_ch: 感谢老板，感谢老板！下次我一定只用一片叶子——啊不是，下次我一定不敢了！我这就回去上班！
t_en: Thank you, boss! Thank you! Next time I'll only use one leaf... No, wait, I mean I won't do it again! I'll go right now!

t_ch: 果断开除，雷厉风行。
t_en: Fire decisively on the stop.

t_ch: 完了…全完了…如果还有下次，我一定只用一片叶子……
t_en: It's over... It's all over... If I ever get another chance, I'll only use one leaf...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，真的是你？大家都说星穹列车也来二相乐园了，我正打算要找你和三月七玩，没想到会在「酒馆」先碰面。
t_en: {NICKNAME}? It really is you! I heard the Astral Express made its way to Planarcadia, and I was planning to find you and March 7th to hang out. Didn't expect to run into you here at the tavern!

t_ch: 云璃？你怎么在这里？
t_en: Yunli? What are you doing here?

t_ch: 笨，这还用想，我当然是跟着爻老板的玉阙使团一起来的呀！
t_en: Isn't it obvious? I came along with Madam Yao's Yuque delegation!

t_ch: 这可不是小孩子该来的地方。
t_en: This isn't really a place for kids.

t_ch: 那块牌子不是只说不让阿哈和悲悼伶人进来吗？而且，我可是爻老板的玉阙使团成员。
t_en: Didn't that sign only say Aha and the Mourning Actors aren't allowed in? Besides, I'm a member of Madam Yao's Yuque delegation.

t_ch: 师父他老人家都说啦，练万次剑不如行万里路，这回跟着爻老板来增长见识，我想着来都来了，不如多去几个平时见不到的地方，多看点新鲜事。
t_en: Master always says that ten thousand miles of travel beats ten thousand swings of the sword. So I figured, since I'm here, I might as well see a few new places and experience something fresh.

t_ch: 别说，这二相乐园<color=#dbc291ff>特色料狸</color>是真的好吃，我这几天尝了无梦咖啡，狮子头，滋味不输仙舟的琼实鸟串。
t_en: You know, the <color=#dbc291ff>special furdishes</color> here in Planarcadia are amazing! I've tried Dreamless Coffee and meatball these past few days, and they're just as good as Xianzhou's berrypheasant skewers.

t_ch: 黄金告解室的人也都特别好玩，个个都<color=#dbc291ff>很有狸貌</color>，说话又好听……
t_en: The folks at the Golden Confessional are all hilarious. They're <color=#dbc291ff>so bolite</color>, and they talk so smoothly...

t_ch: 特色料狸…？
t_en: Special furdishes...?

t_ch: 很有狸貌…？
t_en: So bolite...?

t_ch: 糟糕，说漏嘴哩！
t_en: Oops, slip of the tongue!

t_ch: 哼哼，居然被老板识破了，我只好在此奉上本名——不讲狸！
t_en: Heh heh, looks like you've caught me, boss. Guess I'll have to reveal my true name, Fureasonabo!

t_ch: 狸狸我专门休了年假来这里玩，还特意施展了变身术，让这个难得的假期更有节目效果，可惜被老板您打断了。
t_en: I took a vacation to come have fun, even cast a transformation spell to make it more interesting, but you went and interrupted me, boss.

t_ch: 既然老板打断了狸狸的节目，那么就让您自己变成节目的一部分吧！
t_en: Since you've interrupted my act, you'll just have to be part of the show!

t_ch: 可恶…好狠心的老板，我爸爸都没有打过我……
t_en: Ugh, boss, how heartless... even my dad never hit me...

t_ch: 嗯？不对，我是从流水线上画出来的，哪来的爸爸？
t_en: Wait, that's not right. I was drawn on an assembly line, so I don't have a dad!

t_ch: 不讲狸要换个地方思考这些不讲理的问题，再见！想要我回杂志社工作，等年假休完再说哩！
t_en: I must find somewhere to ponder these nonsensical questions. Bye! If you want me back at the magazine, wait until my vacation's over!

t_ch: 嗯嗯，照相也很需要节奏感呢！
t_en: Mhm, taking photos really is all about rhythm!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，老板抓不到我！
t_en: Hehe, you'll never catch me, boss!

t_ch: 啊…还是被抓到啦。老板老板，我这个变成路障的想法是不是很有创意？快夸夸我！
t_en: Ah... you caught me again. Boss, boss, isn't my idea of turning into a roadblock super creative? Say something nice!

t_ch: 哦，原来你也是杂志社的…
t_en: Oh, so you work for the magazine too...

t_ch: 是啊是啊。怎么样，我是不是比其他员工更有创意？
t_en: Yup! So, what do you think? I'm way more creative than the others, right?

t_ch: 老板？谁是你的老板？
t_en: Boss? Who is your boss?
...Huh?

t_ch: 哦，没事没事，不用在意！在这小巷子里，您我不是我的老板，我也不是您的员工，咱们就是玩了一场捉迷藏而已！
t_en: Oh, don't worry about it! Here in this alley, you're not my boss, and I'm not your employee. We just had a round of hide-and-seek!

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿嘿，感谢老板，咱们下回再见~
t_en: Hehe, thanks, boss. Let's do this again sometime~

t_ch: 少废话，快回去上班。
t_en: Enough excuses. Back to work.

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿嘿，大人不记小人过，老板您最好了。咱们下次再见~
t_en: Heheheh, please forgive me. You're the best, boss. See you next time~

t_ch: 别闹了，快回去上班吧。
t_en: Enough. Get back to work.

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿嘿，大人不记小人过，老板您最好了。咱们下次再见~
t_en: Heheheh, please forgive me. You're the best, boss. See you next time~

t_ch: 你被开除了！
t_en: You're fired!

t_ch: …啊？老板老板，你一定是开玩笑的吧？不是真的要开除我吧？
t_en: ...Huh? Boss, boss, that's a joke, right? You're not actually firing me, are you?

t_ch: 慈悲为怀，拿捏狸心。
t_en: I'll be merciful, if only to gain furbo allegiance.

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿嘿，大人不记小人过，老板您最好了。咱们下次再见~
t_en: Heheheh, please forgive me. You're the best, boss. See you next time~

t_ch: 果断开除，雷厉风行。
t_en: Fire decisively on the stop.

t_ch: 呜呜呜呜呜，老板是大坏蛋！你、你给我等着！！咱、咱、咱们走着瞧！！！
t_en: Waaaah! Boss, you're the worst! Y—You... L—Let's just wait and see!!

t_ch: 我是一朵安静的小花，在这里安静地玩耍……
t_en: I'm just a quiet little flower, peacefully having fun here...

t_ch: 揪！
t_en: Pull it out!

t_ch: 所以，即便你是我的老板，像这样把我揪出来也是罪大恶极的！今天就算要从《狸狸周刊》辞职，狸狸也一定要争这一口气！
t_en: So even if you're my boss, dragging me out like this is unforgivable! Even if I have to quit Furbobo Weekly today, I'm not backing down!

t_ch: …我果然是个废物，摸鱼摸不过自己的老板，连打架也打不过自己的老板。
t_en: ...Guess I really am hopeless. Can't even slack off better than my boss, and can't even win against you in a fight.

t_ch: 所以你还是回去上班吧。
t_en: So get back to work.

t_ch: 也对。像我这样的废物，有什么资格在这个角落里安静地翘班？
t_en: You're right. Someone as hopeless as me doesn't deserve to hide in this corner, slacking off.

t_ch: 等着瞧，老板…我总有一天会强大到能够战胜您，然后，然后——心安理得找一个角落继续翘班！
t_en: Just wait, boss... Someday I'll be strong enough to beat you, and then... then I'll slack off in peace!

t_ch: 给你一个辞职的机会。
t_en: I'll give you the chance to quit.

t_ch: 哼。这破记者的工作我早就不想做了，正好就在这里辞职吧。
t_en: Hmph. I've been wanting to quit this lousy reporter job anyway. Might as well do it now.

t_ch: 老板，咱们就此别过。总有一天，我会强大到能够战胜您的！
t_en: Boss, this is goodbye. One day, I'll be strong enough to defeat you!

t_ch: 您的模仿者已经上线~
t_en: Your imitator is now online~

t_ch: 哦吼，让我看看是谁要在树下告白…
t_en: Oho, let's see who's planning to confess under this tree...

t_ch: 嗯？老板，您把我揪出来做什么？啊…难道，您打算告白的对象，其实就是我？
t_en: Huh? Boss, why are you pulling me out? Ah... don't tell me, the one you're confessing to is me?

t_ch: 别自作多情了。
t_en: Don't flatter yourself.

t_ch: 啊，好伤心。果然我们只是赤裸裸的老板和员工的关系。我们之间唯一的信赖，便是电子屏幕上那冷冰冰的信用点……
t_en: Ah, how tragic. So it's true. We're nothing more than boss and employee. The only trust between us lies in those cold credit points on the screen...

t_ch: 老板？谁是你的老板？
t_en: Boss? Who is your boss?

t_ch: 哎呀，老板您真会开玩笑。《狸狸周刊》里您不在的所有群组都已经传开了！说全银河人气排名第一的无名客已经成为了我们的老板！风华绝代的明日之星！
t_en: Oh, boss, you really know how to joke. The whole Furbobo Weekly chat has been buzzing! They say the galaxy's top celebrity, a Nameless, has become our boss! The shining star of tomorrow!

t_ch: 少废话，快回去上班。
t_en: Enough excuses. Back to work.

t_ch: 欸，老板，此言差矣。我这就是在工作嘛！老板你有所不知…只靠报道新闻，咱《狸狸周刊》可无法养活这么多张嘴——所以外快也是很重要的！
t_en: Eh, boss, that's not quite fair. I am working, you know! You probably don't realize... just doing news reports isn't enough to feed everyone at Furbobo Weekly. Side gigs are important too!

t_ch: 少给我油腔滑调。
t_en: Cut the chatter.

t_ch: …是。您批评得对。
t_en: ...Yes. Your criticism is right.

t_ch: 咱过去在江湖上混了太久，想必沾染了些老板您不喜欢的习气。没关系！「秋叶狸」知错就改！咱这就回一趟杂志社，更改这次的工作计划！不过嘛……
t_en: I've been drifting around the galaxy too long, so I probably picked up some habits you don't like. But it's fine! I own up to my mistakes! I'll head back to the office and fix my plan right away! Though...

t_ch: 老板，您以后如果有合适的任务能交给我，考验考验我作风改变得如何…嘿嘿嘿，那就再好不过啦。老板再见~
t_en: Boss, if you ever have a new assignment for me, something to test if I've really changed... hehe, I'd be more than happy. See you, boss~

t_ch: 不错啊，口齿伶俐。
t_en: Not bad. You've got quite the silver tongue.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，感谢老板赞美！老板以后如果有什么特殊任务，能想到我「秋叶狸」的名字那便是再好不过！
t_en: Hehe, thank you, boss! If you ever need someone for a special mission, I hope the name Furliago comes to mind!

t_ch: 只可惜我现在已经暴露，想要追逐流量，只能寻找新的埋伏点啦…老板，咱们有缘再见~
t_en: Too bad I've been exposed. If I want to chase the spotlight, I'll have to find a new hiding spot... until next time, boss~

t_ch: 你好啊！{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Hello! {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你…好…啊？
t_en: Hello?

t_ch: 不错，你不加反应就接受了这个事实——我是多元宇宙的另一个{NICKNAME}，我们势均力敌、敌我难辨、最终在此狭路相逢……
t_en: Impressive. You accepted it without flinching. I'm another version of {NICKNAME} from the multiverse. We're evenly matched, impossible to tell apart, destined to clash here...

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}，这只是个代号，你可以叫{NICKNAME}，我也可以叫{NICKNAME}。」要考考我是不是？这招没用，你脑海中的所有记忆我也同样拥有，因为，我就是你！
t_en: "{NICKNAME}, it's just a codename. You can be {NICKNAME}, and I can be {NICKNAME} too." Oh, testing me, are you? That won't work. Every memory in your head exists in mine too, because I am you!

t_ch: 你是我，那我是谁？
t_en: If you're me, then who am I?

t_ch: 如此哲学的问题，我只能说：你既是我，也是独一无二的你，是由过往的时间和与他人的关联所造就的{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Such a philosophical question. I'd say, you're me, yet you're also you, the one shaped by time and every connection you've made along the way, the one and only {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我这就打开漫游签证改名。
t_en: Guess I'll just go to the Trailblazer Profile and change my name then.

t_ch: 我是专利产品，仿冒必究。
t_en: I'm a registered trademark. Copies will be prosecuted.

t_ch: 所以，你是我的替身使者？
t_en: So you're... my substitute?

t_ch: 哦？你的反应真是太有趣了，哈哈哈哈哈哈……
t_en: Oh? That reaction's priceless. Hahahahahaha...

t_ch: 你突然爆发出一阵得意的大笑，让你摸不着头脑，你看着和你一个模子里刻出来的你，开始思考你其实只是这个世界里的通用模组NPC的可能性……
t_en: You suddenly burst into laughter, leaving yourself puzzled. You stare at the version of you that looks exactly the same and start wondering if you're just another stock NPC in this world...

t_ch: 老板竟然没有发现吗？在下的变身术真是出神入化哩！请好好看着我，我是《狸狸周刊》的一线员工「倒影狸」啊！
t_en: You didn't notice that, boss? My transformation spell is impeccable! Look closely. I'm Fureflecto, a top reporter from Furbobo Weekly!

t_ch: 老板没听说过吗，一个公司最理想的状态，就是有没有老板都一样能够运转。既然您成为了《狸狸周刊》的新老板，在下当然要尝试cos一下您啦。
t_en: Haven't you heard, boss? The best kind of company is one that runs perfectly well even without its boss. Now that you've taken over Furbobo Weekly, I just had to try cosplaying as you.

t_ch: 形象上的cos看起来已经毫无问题…下一步就是检验战斗力了。来吧老板！看看在下究竟有没有资格成为您的模仿者！
t_en: The look is spot-on... next comes the strength test. Come on, boss! Let's see if I'm worthy of being your imitator!

t_ch: …唉，战斗力的差距果然有够悬殊。看来要成为以假乱真的模仿者，在下只能有意回避需要展示武力的情况了。
t_en: ...*sigh* The power gap's way too big. Looks like I'll have to stay away from situations that involve fighting if I ever want to pass as the real deal.

t_ch: 咦…等等。或许还有一种解法？如果在下从今天开始锻炼武艺，那么十年之后，在下和老板究竟谁更强大也很难说吧？
t_en: Hmm... maybe there's another way? If I start training now, who knows who'll be stronger in ten years, you or me?

t_ch: 别做梦了。
t_en: Stop dreaming!

t_ch: …也是。十年之后，在下会不会已经退化成了尘灵，好像也很难说啊。
t_en: ...Fair point. In ten years, I might have degenerated into a wispae by then.

t_ch: 不过——只要老板需要，在下还是很乐意cos成您的样子帮您解决问题！今天让您知道了《狸狸周刊》有「倒影狸」这么一个员工，在下已经很知足啦！拜拜！
t_en: Still, boss, if you ever need help, I'll gladly take your form and handle things for you! I'm happy enough that you now know Furbobo Weekly has an employee named Fureflecto! Bye-bye!

t_ch: 有志者事竟成！
t_en: Where there's a will, there's a way!

t_ch: 好耶！在下这就开始制定锻炼身体的计划，冬练三伏、夏练三九！呃…是不是这话有哪里不对。
t_en: Hooray! Time to start my training! Sweat through summer, freeze through winter! Uh... wait, that doesn't sound quite right.

t_ch: 咳咳，反正，只要老板需要，在下还是很乐意cos成您的样子帮您解决问题！今天让您知道了《狸狸周刊》有「倒影狸」这么一个员工，在下已经很知足啦！拜拜！
t_en: Ahem, anyway, if you ever need help, I'll gladly take your form and handle things for you! I'm happy enough that you now know Furbobo Weekly has an employee named Fureflecto! Bye-bye!

t_ch: 想从这个角度看到我可不容易~
t_en: It's not easy to get me to look at things from this angle.

t_ch: 这是一柄云骑军制式的阵刀，刀脊上不时流过森冷的青光，锋锐逼人，吹毛断发。
t_en: This is a Cloud Knight's standard-issue Devastator Glaive. There's a cold glint on the spine of the blade sometimes. You get the feeling that it could cut steel like butter.

t_ch: 刀孤零零地靠在墙角，好像一名依然雄姿英发的战士蜷缩在布满灰尘的角落里乞讨，令人心生怜惜。
t_en: The blade leaned against the corner alone, like a once-majestic warrior curled up in a dusty corner begging for alms and looking piteous.

t_ch: 是谁，何时，又为什么要弃置这样一柄刀器？
t_en: Who, when, and why was such a weapon abandoned?

t_ch: （拿起来耍一耍）
t_en: (Pick it up and play around with it)

t_ch: 你见惯了云骑军手执阵刀的威风模样，便有样学样擎住刀柄，信手一挥。
t_en: You've seen enough Cloud Knights brandishing their Devastator Glaives around, so you hold the handle and swing it around with ease.

t_ch: 没有破风声，只有金属坠地的碎响。
t_en: There's no swishing sound, just that of metal clanging onto the ground.

t_ch: （算了吧）
t_en: (Forget about it)

t_ch: 刀的长度显然超过了你的身高，你思忖了片刻，还是放过自己吧。
t_en: The weapon is longer than your height. You mull it over for a second before deciding to spare yourself.

t_ch: 我、我的刀！
t_en: M—My blade!

t_ch: 一个男人从你身后现身，带着心痛如绞的表情，虽然表情中多了几分例行公事的意思。
t_en: A man appears behind you, wearing a pained expression, but something in his expression betrays a sense of practice.

t_ch: 我的祖传宝刀！怎地被你伤成了这样？！你、你得赔我！
t_en: My heirloom sword! How could you damage it like this!? P—Pay me back!

t_ch: 哦豁，来了来了。每到一个世界，若不能体会一次当地的街头骗术，感受一下人性中的黑暗面，那简直是白跑了一趟。
t_en: Oh, there it comes! Every time I go off-world, I'll feel like it's a waste of time if I can't find any street cheats and experience a little about the dark side of humanity.

t_ch: 仿佛被你一脸无所谓的神气激怒，男人也变得认真起来。
t_en: The man grows serious, as if angered by your indifferent expression.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，你怕是不知道打造一把云骑阵刀要花费多少工夫？！
t_en: {F#Girl}{M#Boy}, do you realize how much effort goes into crafting one of these Devastator Glaives for the Cloud Knights?

t_ch: 随后他便扯些什么「动力武器」、「单分子刀刃」之类的专业词汇。街角上下顿时充满了学术的空气。
t_en: Then he spouted industrial jargon such as "propulsion weapon" and "monomolecular blade," filling the surrounding street with a hint of academia.

t_ch: 所以，你打算怎么赔我？
t_en: So how are you gonna pay me back, then?

t_ch: 你的刀？你叫一声，看刀应不应你？
t_en: Your blade? Call it, and see if it answers you.

t_ch: 你这厮想混赖！这刀靠近刀锷处镌了先祖父名讳一个「泉」字，这应该可以证明是我家传之物了吧！
t_en: You rascal! This sword is engraved with my ancestor's name on it — the character Quan, meaning wellspring. This should prove that it is beyond a doubt, my family heirloom!

t_ch: 你凭啥在我的刀上刻字？
t_en: Why have you engraved a character on my sword?

t_ch: 确实，我在刀上刻了个「泉」字。
t_en: That's right, I myself engraved the character Quan on it.

t_ch: 被你理直气壮的发言所震慑，男人一时竟无言以对。
t_en: Dumbstruck by your righteous statement, the man remains speechless for a moment.

t_ch: 好你个{F#小姑娘}{M#臭小子}，还敢强词夺理。看来，今天不好好教训你一番，你是长不了记性了。
t_en: Don't get smart with me, {F#missy}{M#scoundrel}! Seems like I'll have to teach you a little lesson here that you won't soon forget.

t_ch: 你就这么静静站着看他。男人脸上一阵青一阵白，仿佛在评估着彼此的实力。
t_en: You just stand there in silence, looking on. The man's face turns blue and then white, as if sizing you up.

t_ch: 最后，陌生的男人向后退去，消失在街角。
t_en: Finally, the stranger backs off and disappears into the streets.

t_ch: 吹半天这刀是瓷打的么？一碰就碎。
t_en: What is this blade made of, anyway? Ceramic? It's so fragile.

t_ch: 一看你就是刚来仙舟的化外民，也不怪你。这长生种的东西，往少了说都得有个八百年的历史！
t_en: I can see you must be new around here, Outworlder, so a little history lesson. A long-life species possession like this has a history of at least eight hundred years!

t_ch: 在你眼里它是一把刀。可在我眼里，它是一件文物，冲锋陷阵，历尽风霜，斩过丰饶孽物，斩过钢铁劲敌。谁能想到，如今它的命运，却是折在了一个什么都不懂的小毛孩手里！
t_en: To you, it may look like any ordinary blade. But in our eyes, it's a cultural relic that's broken enemy lines. It fought through centuries of vicissitudes, slain the Abominations of Abundance, as well as many a valiant enemy. Who could ever imagine its noble fate being in the hands of such an ignorant little pipsqueak!

t_ch: 快点，我赶时间，你准备出多少？
t_en: C'mon, I'm in a hurry... How much can you pay in damages?

t_ch: 我们来问问云骑军…
t_en: Let's ask the Cloud Knights...

t_ch: 这么珍贵的文物怎么搁在街边？
t_en: How can such a precious relic just be left on the street like this?

t_ch: 被你切中要害的发言所震慑，男人一时竟无言以对。
t_en: Your straight-to-the-point remarks shocked the man, rendering him speechless for a moment.

t_ch: 好你个{F#小姑娘}{M#臭小子}，还敢强词夺理。看来，今天不好好教训你一番，你是长不了记性了。
t_en: Don't get smart with me, {F#missy}{M#scoundrel}! Seems like I'll have to teach you a little lesson here that you won't soon forget.

t_ch: 你就这么静静站着看他。男人脸上一阵青一阵白，仿佛意识到自己说错了话。
t_en: You just stand there in silence, looking on. The man's face turns blue and then white, as if realizing the mistake in what he'd just said.

t_ch: 最后，陌生的男人向后退去，消失在街角。
t_en: Finally, the stranger backs off and disappears into the streets.

t_ch: 说罢，多少钱？
t_en: Enough, how much do you want?

t_ch: …你就不反驳两句？亏我还满期待你能说些什么。
t_en: ...Aren't you even going to fight me a couple of times? Here I am, expecting you to come back at me with something.

t_ch: 不用了，不就是钱么？
t_en: Nothing, really — it's just money.

t_ch: 别磨磨唧唧的，赶时间！
t_en: Quit dawdling! I'm in a hurry.

t_ch: 男人一时间竟没有料到事情可以展开的如此顺利。他皱眉半晌，摊开手掌。
t_en: The man didn't expect everything to have gone so smoothly. He frowns and spreads open his palms.

t_ch: 一口价，五百信用点！
t_en: <unbreak>500</unbreak> credits!

t_ch: 拿着，别再干这行了。
t_en: Take it. Just quit doing this.

t_ch: 谢、谢谢{F#大姐}{M#大爷}。
t_en: Th—Thank you, {F#milady}{M#milord}!

t_ch: 男人带着一脸不可思议的表情，收下钱，迅速消失在街角。
t_en: He looks at you incredulously, before taking the money and scarpering off around the corner.

t_ch: 不行，二百五十！一口价！
t_en: No, <unbreak>250</unbreak>! That's my price!

t_ch: （咬牙）二百五就二百五！
t_en: *clench teeth* <unbreak>250</unbreak> it is!

t_ch: 男人带着一脸不可思议的表情，收下钱，迅速消失在街角。
t_en: He looks at you incredulously, before taking the money and scarpering off around the corner.

t_ch: 我被碰瓷了
t_en: I've been scammed.

t_ch: 这个男人的声音分外耳熟，你仿佛在哪里听过？
t_en: This man's voice seems very familiar to you. Where have you heard it before?

t_ch: 是你小子！
t_en: There you are, punk!

t_ch: 咱、咱俩见过？
t_en: H—Have we met before?

t_ch: 男人勉力回忆，仿佛穷汉想从空空如也的钱袋里刮出几枚救急钱。
t_en: He tried his best to recall, as though he were a beggar scraping out the last coins from his empty wallet.

t_ch: 虽说冤有头债有主，但咱碰瓷过的苦主数不胜数，记不得了…
t_en: Every action must be held accountable, yes, but I'm afraid I've lost count of the number of people I've run this scam on...

t_ch: 没事，反正我也没上当。
t_en: That's okay, I didn't get scammed anyway.

t_ch: 我不是给了钱，让你改过自新吗？
t_en: Didn't I give you some money so you could turn over a new leaf?

t_ch: 啥？
t_en: Wuh?

t_ch: 他再次搜肠刮肚，老半天才回过神来。
t_en: He racks his brains once again, and eventually comes up with a solution after what seems like ages.

t_ch: 果然人世间所有的分离都是久别重逢啊。我当时就琢磨呢，行骗生涯里还没遇到过那么轻轻松松就把钱给掏了的人，不会是阔人家的傻{F#女儿}{M#儿子}吧……
t_en: You know what they say, every farewell is just an eventual reunion. At the time I was thinking, I've never met anyone in my whole career, who's given up money to me so easily. Surely this young {F#lady}{M#man} must be either wealthy or foolish...

t_ch: 改过自新…原来是这个意思吗？但是才给几百块够干啥的？怎么都得给个万把块启动资金吧？
t_en: Turn over a new leaf... Is that what that means? All I've got is a couple of hundred measly credits. What's that good for? You should've at least given me some ten-grand start-up fund, no?

t_ch: 您看这样，我呢，眼下被地衡司的执事给逮了。要不您给我交个保，再接济点儿。我以后就跟您混了。
t_en: How about this, then — I got caught by the Realm-Keeping Commission. If you help post my bail and help me out a little, you can be my boss.

t_ch: 别看我这样，我可是啥都能干！剪绺碰瓷打边鼓那是样样精通。
t_en: Don't look at me like that. I can do anything! Pickpocketing, runnin' scams, backing up the boss... I'm the best flunky around.

t_ch: 不是，您别走啊…
t_en: No, don't go...

t_ch: （放弃吧，想不起来了）
t_en: (Forget it. I can't remember)

t_ch: 我被碰瓷了。
t_en: I've been scammed.

t_ch: 你把在街边被碰瓷经历详细说了一遍。
t_en: Please recount for me your entire experience of getting scammed in the street.

t_ch: 是受害者没跑了。这厮惯常拿什么「祖传宝刀」骗外来的旅客。受害人对仙舟不熟悉，就很容易着了他的道。
t_en: You must be the victim. This guy used the old "Family Treasure Sword" scam to trick Outworlders all the time. The victims are unfamiliar with Xianzhou history, and so are easily tricked.

t_ch: 那么，受害金额？
t_en: What then was the amount lost?

t_ch: 没有损失。
t_en: No losses.

t_ch: 很机警啊。下次遇到什么情况，记得向您身边的地衡司人员求助。
t_en: Very astute. Next time when you encounter a situation, remember to seek help from your nearby Realm-Keeping Commission officer.

t_ch: <unbreak>250</unbreak>信用点。
t_en: <unbreak>250</unbreak> Credits.

t_ch: 退赔的钱您拿好了。下次遇到这种情况，先别急着给钱。记得向您身边的地衡司人员求助。
t_en: Here's for your reparations. Next time if you encounter situations like this, please don't give money in a panic. Remember to seek help from your nearby Realm-Keeping Commission officer.

t_ch: <unbreak>500</unbreak>信用点。
t_en: <unbreak>500</unbreak> Credits.

t_ch: 退赔的钱您拿好了。下次遇到这种情况，先别急着给钱。记得向您身边的地衡司人员求助。
t_en: Here's for your reparations. Next time if you encounter situations like this, please don't give money in a panic. Remember to seek help from your nearby Realm-Keeping Commission officer.

t_ch: <unbreak>5000</unbreak>信用点！
t_en: <unbreak>5000</unbreak> Credits!

t_ch: 被骗了这么多？要说怎么骗子不够用呢……
t_en: That much!? Not even a scammer could spend that much...

t_ch: 不是，我是说您得提高警觉性了。
t_en: No, I'm saying that you should be more alert.

t_ch: 执事狠狠看了身边的骗子一眼。
t_en: The officer ruthlessly scrutinizes the surrounding scammers.

t_ch: 下次遇到这种情况，先别急着给钱。记得向您身边的地衡司人员求助。
t_en: Next time if you encounter situations like this, please don't give money in a panic. Remember to seek help from your nearby Realm-Keeping Commission officer.

t_ch: 你是受害者？姓名，事由，受害金额？
t_en: Are you the victim? State your name, occupation, and amount in losses.

t_ch: 就在几息之前，一位仙舟人望着屏幕，手指不断滑动着。
t_en: Just a few seconds ago, a Xianzhou man was peering at the screen and sliding his finger around on it.

t_ch: 你注意到他呼吸粗重，面色潮红，显然屏幕上有些「了不得」的玩意儿令他兴奋不已。
t_en: You notice his heavy breathing, his face flushed, and he's clearly excited by something "amazing" on the screen.

t_ch: 然而这人不知为何突然拂袖离去，却留下了摊开着的展阅，光幕中不断跃动着引人遐思的信息。
t_en: However, for some unknown reason, this person leaves without so much as an indication — leaving behind his flexible display for all to see with its constantly scrolling data on-screen.

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 有点好奇，上去看看。
t_en: Interesting. Go take a look.

t_ch: 你注意到最上方有一行大字，字体周围着炫酷的光效：「退伍云骑，深夜倾情，专属热线——用最坚硬的盔甲，点燃你的心。」
t_en: You notice a line of large characters at the top, glowing with a cool sparkly font: "Retired Cloud Knight for late-night romance; your exclusive hotline — Let only the hardest armor ignite your passions."

t_ch: 你完全不明白云骑军的盔甲为什么能用作引燃物。但想起那位仙舟人先前驻足于此时的神情，你决定——
t_en: You have absolutely no idea how Cloud Knights' armor could be used as an ignition device. But thinking back to the Xianzhou man who stopped here and his facial expression, you decide...

t_ch: 好怪，再看一眼。
t_en: Strange, let's take another look.

t_ch: 这里的审美与爱好真是匪夷所思，令你咂舌称奇。
t_en: The aesthetics and pastimes here are simply bizarre and leave you gobsmacked.

t_ch: 惊叹之余，你毅然决定点开看看，好奇心指引着你探索仙舟上幽邃隐秘却最为地道的风土人情。
t_en: While amazed, you decisively tap it open and take a look. Your curiosity guides you to explore the most secret and profound of authentic local customs.

t_ch: 太吓人了，赶紧关掉。
t_en: Absolutely freaky. Close it, immediately.

t_ch: 这里的审美与爱好真是太过吓人，超出了你所能接受的底线。
t_en: The aesthetics and pastimes here are simply terrifying, and stretch far beyond your bottom line.

t_ch: 炫酷的光效多看几秒更觉刺眼，眼不见为净，还是把页面关上为好。
t_en: The sparkly visual on the screen glows even more glaring the longer you gaze at it. It's best to see no evil and close the page for now.

t_ch: 你的指尖刚与展阅相接触，光幕上争先恐后地涌出了一系列声色并茂的页面——
t_en: Just as your fingertips touch the flexible display, a flood of colors and sounds spurts out of page on the display...

t_ch: 画面中出现了几位身披重甲、手持蛇矛的狐人少女，她们眼神坚毅，口中念念有词：
t_en: Several foxian girls wearing heavy armor and holding serpent spears appear on-screen. Their look is staunch as they quietly mutter:

t_ch: 「每晚定时，大姐带队。新区战斗随便打，装备高价回收，巡镝马上到账。」
t_en: "Each night, your queen leads her army. We fight through the new zone for epic loot, and pay out in strales immediately."

t_ch: 「是姐妹，就来跟我！」
t_en: "With me, sisters!"

t_ch: 看着这则别有风味的仙舟广告，你决定——
t_en: After seeing this unique Xianzhou advert, you decide to...

t_ch: 点进去看看。
t_en: Check it out.

t_ch: 太怪了，赶紧关掉。
t_en: It's weird, close it, quick.

t_ch: 你确信自己并没有点错，但展阅上却飞速弹出了几个与你想法背道而驰的页面——
t_en: You're sure you've hit the right thing, but several pop-up pages that run counter to your thoughts start showing up on the flexible display...

t_ch: 「『他终于肯低头认错了？』『小姐，不好了，少爷藏在流云渡的货运船舱里逃了！』『哼，我太卜司自有办法推衍，我倒要看看他能去哪儿。』她追，他逃，他插翅难逃！」
t_en: "'Does he finally acknowledge his wrongs?''Miss! Bad news! The young master ran away by hiding inside a cargo container in Cloudford!''Hmph, we have methods of deduction at the Divination Commission. I wonder where he can possibly go.' She chases, he flees, and he can't escape!"

t_ch: 「『退婚？你…你们究竟是何人…我失去的一切，我一定要亲手拿回来！』丹鼎司天才医师昏迷数载，醒来竟遭未婚夫退婚羞辱！且看她如何逆转败局，夺回失去的一切！」
t_en: " 'Call off the marriage? Y—You... Who are you to... I've lost everything, and I'm going to claw it back with my bare hands!' The prodigy healer from the Alchemy Commission fell into a coma for several years, and awoke to the humiliating news of her fiancé's broken engagement! A thrilling story of reversing defeat and reclamation!"

t_ch: 「『妹妹，为什么丹鼎司的司鼎长得如此像你？』『什么？哥哥竟是天才飞行士？』『工造司的百冶登门造访，竟要给我贴身造甲？』一场交易，让他加入云骑离家数载。归来之时家中竟有大变，就连看似柔弱的未婚妻，竟也是个了不得的大人物！」
t_en: "'Sis, why do you look so similar to the Cauldron Master of the Alchemy Commission?' 'What? My brother is a genius pilot?' 'The Artisanship Commission's Furnace Master has come to visit and wants to make a personalized suit of armor for me?' A transaction made him join the Cloud Knights and leave home for several years. Upon his return, dramatic changes had happened in his family, and even his seemingly frail fiancee is a big shot!"

t_ch: 看上去似乎是仙舟上极为流行的话本，但从内容上看，你开始怀疑到底是什么人在看这种东西。你决定——
t_en: It seems like it's a very popular Xianzhou story, but judging by the content, you wonder what kind of people would actually read this thing. You decide...

t_ch: 让我看看！
t_en: Let me see!

t_ch: 不堪入目，赶紧关掉。
t_en: How unsightly. Shut it off, now.

t_ch: 你正准备按下去，耳畔却突然响起一声呵斥——
t_en: You were about to press the button when a reprimanding voice caught your attention...

t_ch: 帮他合上展阅。
t_en: Help him close the flexible display.

t_ch: 显然这是那位仙舟人落在这儿的东西，私人物品暴露在公开场合，难免不太好。
t_en: Obviously, this is what the Xianzhou man left behind. It's not a good idea to expose his personal belongings in public's view.

t_ch: 你保住了一位陌生人的尊严。
t_en: You have preserved the dignity of a stranger.
Oiiii!

t_ch: 我说你，怎么这么没规矩，乱翻别人的展阅。
t_en: How are you so rude as to going through someone else's flexible display?

t_ch: 我不是故意的，我只是好奇。
t_en: It was an accident, and I was just curious.

t_ch: 要讲规矩！不问自取这可不好！
t_en: Be disciplined! It's not a good habit to pick something up without asking!

t_ch: 我想帮你关上但关不掉…
t_en: I wanted to turn it off for you, but I couldn't...

t_ch: …难道你也…我明白了。
t_en: ...So you... I see...

t_ch: 不是，你怎么这么…哦，我懂了，看你这打扮，应该是化外民吧。
t_en: No, how could you... Ack, I see. Judging by your outfit, you're an Outworlder, right?

t_ch: 好吧，是这么回事——这东西就跟狗皮膏药似的，你看到就别乱碰，不然这种「垃圾消息」只会越来越多。
t_en: Alright, here's what going on... This thing is like a strong adhesive. Whatever you do, don't touch it, otherwise this spam news will just keep growing.

t_ch: 我刚刚就是发现忘记拿展阅了，才特意回来一趟。
t_en: I just realized that I forgot to take back my flexible display, so I came back to do that.

t_ch: 我得把这东西带去修一下，修不好就扔了，省得污人眼睛。
t_en: I have to take this thing in to get fixed, or throw it out otherwise — so as not to contaminate anyone else's vision.

t_ch: …什么意思？
t_en: ...What do you mean?

t_ch: …就这个意思啊？这东西坏了，这种垃圾消息到处都是，我就拿去修一下咯，修不好就扔了呗！
t_en: ...I mean, exactly what I just said? This thing is junk, and these spam messages get out all over the place. Let me take it to get it fixed, or I'll just throw it out!

t_ch: 扔？我以为是你故意看的…
t_en: Throw it out? I thought you were watching it...

t_ch: 停停停，你把我当什么人了！
t_en: Hold on, wait, what kind of person do you take me for?

t_ch: 哎，也怪我，当时借给邻居家的狐人小孩玩了一会儿。结果送回来的时候就多了一些乱七八糟的东西……
t_en: *sigh* It's my fault. I lent it to the neighbor's foxian kid to play with. When I came back, it was stuffed full of this junk...

t_ch: 怎么清理都清不掉，还越弹越多。
t_en: I can't wipe any of it, and if I try, more of them would just pop up.
